nodes.label: make-app.sh
nodes.rationale: Zsh packaging script that builds NotchAgent.app from the SwiftPM release binary without an Xcode project. Assembles Contents/MacOS + Contents/Resources, writes Info.plist, generates the .icns icon set, code-signs the bundle with a stable local Apple Development identity, and reports the output size. Comment header now explicitly notes signing is local (Apple Development) for Keychain ACL persistence across rebuilds, distinct from Developer ID + notarization needed for public distribution. Current VERSION=1.0.1.

nodes.label: make-app.sh script (main routine)
nodes.rationale: Top-to-bottom entry routine: kill running NotchAgent instances -> swift build -c release -> assemble .app bundle skeleton -> write Info.plist (CFBundleShortVersionString=${VERSION}, now 1.0.1) -> generate AppIcon.icns via Scripts/gen-icon.swift + sips/iconutil -> codesign --force --deep -s "$SIGN_IDENTITY" "$APP" -> print bundle size and install hint.

nodes.label: Code Signing
nodes.rationale: codesign --force --deep -s "$SIGN_IDENTITY" "$APP" signs the assembled bundle. Previously ran ad-hoc (`codesign -s -`); now signs with a named local Apple Development identity.

nodes.label: Apple Development Identity (K74FG72F9W / Team S3YCFYY8SC)
nodes.rationale: SIGN_IDENTITY="Apple Development: luis roquette (K74FG72F9W)" — a local, non-ad-hoc code signing identity with a fixed Team ID (S3YCFYY8SC). Chosen specifically because it gives codesign a stable identity across rebuilds, unlike ad-hoc signing.

nodes.label: Keychain ACL Persistence (Always Allow grant)
nodes.rationale: macOS Keychain grants access ('Always Allow') to a keychain item scoped by the signing identity of the requesting app. With ad-hoc signing there is no Team ID, so the ACL identifier is derived from the binary's own content hash; every rebuild changes that hash, so macOS treats each build as a new app and invalidates the prior grant for the 'Claude Code-credentials' item, forcing a repeated password prompt. Signing with a fixed identity (stable Team ID) keeps the ACL identifier constant across rebuilds, so a single 'Always Allow' grant persists.

nodes.label: Ad-hoc → Stable Identity Signing Fix
nodes.rationale: make-app.sh used to sign the packaged .app with `codesign -s -` (ad-hoc). Ad-hoc signing has no Team ID, so its Keychain ACL identifier is derived from the binary's own content hash — every rebuild produced what macOS Keychain considered a 'new app', silently invalidating any prior 'Always Allow' grant for the 'Claude Code-credentials' item and forcing a repeated password prompt on each run. The fix replaces ad-hoc signing with a named local identity, SIGN_IDENTITY="Apple Development: luis roquette (K74FG72F9W)", giving a fixed Team ID (S3YCFYY8SC) so a single 'Always Allow' grant now persists across rebuilds. Shipped together with a VERSION bump to 1.0.1, released as GitHub release v1.0.1.

nodes.label: NotchAgent v1.0.1 release
nodes.rationale: VERSION="1.0.1" bumped in the same fix that switched code signing from ad-hoc to a stable Apple Development identity; published as GitHub release v1.0.1.

nodes.label: App Bundle Assembly
nodes.rationale: Recreates dist/NotchAgent.app, creates Contents/MacOS and Contents/Resources, copies the release binary in, and heredocs a hand-written Info.plist (bundle id br.com.lfrprojects.notchagent, CFBundleShortVersionString=${VERSION}, LSUIElement app, min system 14.0).

nodes.label: Icon Generation
nodes.rationale: Generates a base 512x512@2x PNG via Scripts/gen-icon.swift, downsamples it with sips into the full iconset size matrix (16..512, @1x/@2x), then packs the .iconset into AppIcon.icns with iconutil for Contents/Resources.
